headline: Malaysia Arrests 3 Suspects in Nuclear Material Smuggling
context: PDRM and IAEA cooperation led to arrest of 3 suspects in Penang attempting to sell enriched uranium material in February 2026.

cards.big: 3 arrested in Penang attempting to sell enriched uranium material
cards.sub: Joint PDRM-IAEA operation intercepted 1.2kg of material traced to Central Asia

cards.big: Suspects attempted sale to undercover agents posing as intermediaries
cards.sub: Material classified as low-enriched insufficient for weapons but radiation hazard

cards.big: Interpol red notice led to arrests after 8-month investigation across 4 countries
cards.sub: Malaysia ratified IAEA Additional Protocol in 2023 enabling deeper cooperation

cards.big: Case revealed Malaysia as transit point for illicit nuclear material networks
cards.sub: Government tightened port radiation scanning from 15% to 45% of containers

cards.big: Nuclear smuggling tests whether Malaysia can secure itself against unconventional threats
3 nuclear material smuggling arrests. Non-proliferation enforcement requires specialised capacity, not general policing.
